title: Molecular Data of a Novel Penoulivirus Associated with the Plant-Pathogenic Fungus Erysiphe necator
author: Galán-Cubero, Rocío
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Erysiphe necator  is a very important biotrophic fungus responsible for grapevine powdery mildew. A novel mycovirus named Erysiphe necator-associated ourmia-like virus 21 (EnaOLV21), has been identified and molecularly characterized for the first time in this study. A partial sequence of the virus was first identified by next-generation sequencing (NGS) in pools of  E. necator  isolates infecting grapevine. Its terminal sequences were determined by the RNA ligase-mediated rapid amplification of cDNA ends (RLM-RACE) technique and RNA folding computational models were generated by MFOLD software. Finally, the full-length sequence was obtained by cloning and by Sanger sequencing in the RDPM33  E. necator  isolate. EnaOLV21 has a 2,680-nucleotide (nt) sequence which contains open reading frames (ORFs) in both senses coding for a putative RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) and three other putative proteins. Phylogenetic analyses grouped EnaOLV21 into a clade that includes members of the genus  Penoulivirus  within the family  Botourmiaviridae . This is the first penoulivirus with hypothetical proteins putatively encoded in the negative strand. This study collects the first complete molecular data of a mycovirus associated with the fungus  E. necator , an important grapevine pathogen.
